title: maesa costulata
intro: Maesa costulata, also known as the Ceylon boxwood or wild boxwood, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Asia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Maesa costulata include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and even exhibit anti-cancer properties. In terms of herbal preparations, Maesa costulata is often used in the form of tea, infused in water, or extracted as a tincture to leverage its medicinal properties.
